name: r2
context: Upload, download, list, and manage objects in Cloudflare R2 — S3-compatible storage with zero egress fees
info: Cloudflare R2 is S3-compatible object storage with zero egress fees. It supports the S3 API, so existing S3 tools (AWS CLI, Boto3, aws-sdk) work with R2 by changing the endpoint. R2 is accessed from Workers via bindings or externally via the S3-compatible API. Objects can be up to 5TB. R2 supports multipart uploads, conditional headers (ETags), custom metadata, and public bucket access via r2.dev domains or custom domains. Common uses: file hosting, backups, media storage, static assets, and data lakes. R2 is strongly consistent — reads after writes always return the latest data.
triggers: set up s3 compatible storage on cloudflare

sections.title: Creating buckets and bindings
sections.body: Create a bucket with `wrangler r2 bucket create <name>`. Add an `[[r2_buckets]]` binding in `wrangler.toml` with `binding`, `bucket_name`, and optional `preview_bucket_name`. The binding exposes an `R2Bucket` object on the Worker's `env`. For S3 API access, generate R2 API tokens in the dashboard.

sections.title: Object CRUD operations
sections.body: `env.MY_BUCKET.put(key, body, options?)` uploads an object. Body can be string, ArrayBuffer, ReadableStream, Blob, or null. Options include `httpMetadata` (contentType, cacheControl) and `customMetadata` (key-value pairs). `env.MY_BUCKET.get(key)` returns an R2ObjectBody with `.body` (ReadableStream), `.key`, `.size`, `.etag`. `env.MY_BUCKET.delete(key)` or `env.MY_BUCKET.delete([keys])` for batch delete.

sections.title: S3 API compatibility
sections.body: R2 implements the S3 API. Use any S3 client (AWS CLI, @aws-sdk/client-s3, boto3) by changing the endpoint to `https://<ACCOUNT_ID>.r2.cloudflarestorage.com`. Requires R2 API credentials (Access Key ID + Secret Access Key), not Cloudflare API tokens. Generate credentials in Dashboard > R2 > Manage R2 API Tokens.

sections.title: Public bucket access
sections.body: Enable public access via r2.dev subdomain in the dashboard (Settings > Public access). Objects are served at `https://pub-<hash>.r2.dev/<key>`. For custom domains, connect a domain via the dashboard. Public buckets serve objects without authentication. Use a Worker in front of the bucket for access control.
